extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 10km remaining on penultimate climb, used teammates to control, then beat sprinters Matthews/Pidcock in reduced bunch finish for yellow.
tactical_patterns: Pogačar attacked 10km out not to solo but to shed deadweight, regrouped teammates, converted chaos to controlled sprint finish.
Wout van Aert went in 85km break while wearing yellow, unusually aggressive jersey defense that failed mechanically early.
Peloton refused to grant break breathing room despite 220km distance, signaling pre-mountain stage GC positioning obsession trumped energy conservation.

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: negative: mechanically failed in break he created while in yellow, immediately dropped on first climb after repair
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: converted late attack into sprint with teammates assembled behind, proving polyvalent threat even on non-mountain stages
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: survived full-gas pace described as one of fastest 220km stages in Tour history to contest finale
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: launched early sprint 300m out but caught by stronger Pogačar, positioning decent but no finishing punch
rider_signals.POWLESS Neilson: positive: survived to take yellow jersey virtually before Pogačar's bonus seconds reversed it, UAE allowing dangerman proximity
